units.unit_name: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Matter, Physical World, Living World
topics: CG 1 – Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level

C 1.1 – Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table, and explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on the atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency).

C 1.2 – Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions)
C 1.3 – Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid and base, metal and non-metal, reversible and irreversible)

units.unit_name: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
topics: CG 2 – Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis

C 2.1 – Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces (change in state of motion — displacement and direction, velocity and acceleration, uniform circular motion, acceleration due to gravity) and analyses graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension
C 2.2 – Explains the relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation, and connect it to the laws of motion

C 2.3 – Manipulates the position of object and properties of lenses (focus, centre of curvature) to observe image characteristics and correspondence with a ray diagram, and extends this understanding to a combination of lenses (telescope, microscope)

C 2.4 – Manipulates and analyses different characteristics of the circuit (current, voltage, resistance) and mathematises their relationship (Ohm's law), and applies it to everyday usage (electricity bill, short circuit, safety measures)
C 2.5 – Defines work in scientific terms, and represents the relationship between potential and kinetic energy (conservation of energy) in mathematical expressions
C 2.6 – Demonstrates the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines (system of levers and pulleys)
C 2.7 – Describes the origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude) and differences in what we hear as it propagates through different instruments

C 2.8 – Explores interconnected systems and phenomena that support and affect life on Earth (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere and their interrelationships, earth processes, hazards, etc.)
key_concepts: Earth systems (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere)

units.unit_name: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Living World at Cellular Level
topics: CG 3 – Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level

C 3.1 – Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi-permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes

raw_nodes.title: CG 1 – Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level

raw_nodes.source_text: CG 1 – Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level

raw_nodes.title: C 1.1 – Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table, and explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on the atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency).
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Matter, Physical World, Living World
raw_nodes.source_text: C 1.1 – Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table, and explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on the atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency).

raw_nodes.title: C 1.2 – Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions)
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Matter, Physical World, Living World
raw_nodes.source_text: C 1.2 – Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions)

raw_nodes.title: C 1.3 – Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid and base, metal and non-metal, reversible and irreversible)
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Matter, Physical World, Living World
raw_nodes.source_text: C 1.3 – Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid and base, metal and non-metal, reversible and irreversible)

raw_nodes.title: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles

raw_nodes.title: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles

raw_nodes.source_text: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles

raw_nodes.title: CG 2 – Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: CG 2 – Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis

raw_nodes.title: C 2.1 – Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces (change in state of motion — displacement and direction, velocity and acceleration, uniform circular motion, acceleration due to gravity) and analyses graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: C 2.1 – Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces (change in state of motion — displacement and direction, velocity and acceleration, uniform circular motion, acceleration due to gravity) and analyses graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension

raw_nodes.title: C 2.2 – Explains the relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation, and connect it to the laws of motion
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: C 2.2 – Explains the relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation, and connect it to the laws of motion

raw_nodes.title: C 2.3 – Manipulates the position of object and properties of lenses (focus, centre of curvature) to observe image characteristics and correspondence with a ray diagram, and extends this understanding to a combination of lenses (telescope, microscope)
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: C 2.3 – Manipulates the position of object and properties of lenses (focus, centre of curvature) to observe image characteristics and correspondence with a ray diagram, and extends this understanding to a combination of lenses (telescope, microscope)

raw_nodes.title: C 2.4 – Manipulates and analyses different characteristics of the circuit (current, voltage, resistance) and mathematises their relationship (Ohm's law), and applies it to everyday usage (electricity bill, short circuit, safety measures)
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: C 2.4 – Manipulates and analyses different characteristics of the circuit (current, voltage, resistance) and mathematises their relationship (Ohm's law), and applies it to everyday usage (electricity bill, short circuit, safety measures)

raw_nodes.title: C 2.5 – Defines work in scientific terms, and represents the relationship between potential and kinetic energy (conservation of energy) in mathematical expressions
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: C 2.5 – Defines work in scientific terms, and represents the relationship between potential and kinetic energy (conservation of energy) in mathematical expressions

raw_nodes.title: C 2.6 – Demonstrates the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines (system of levers and pulleys)
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: C 2.6 – Demonstrates the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines (system of levers and pulleys)

raw_nodes.title: C 2.7 – Describes the origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude) and differences in what we hear as it propagates through different instruments
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: C 2.7 – Describes the origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude) and differences in what we hear as it propagates through different instruments

raw_nodes.title: C 2.8 – Explores interconnected systems and phenomena that support and affect life on Earth (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere and their interrelationships, earth processes, hazards, etc.)
raw_nodes.parent: Curricular Goals and Competencies - Physical World Principles
raw_nodes.source_text: C 2.8 – Explores interconnected systems and phenomena that support and affect life on Earth (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere and their interrelationships, earth processes, hazards, etc.)

practicals.title: Finding pH of various samples
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.details: Find the pH using pH paper/universal indicator for dilute HCl, NaOH solution, Ethanoic acid, lemon juice, water, and hydrogen carbonate solution.

practicals.title: Properties of acids and bases on reaction with indicators and metals
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.details: Study the properties of HCl & NaOH based on their reaction with litmus solution (blue/red), zinc metal, and solid sodium carbonate.

practicals.title: Classification of chemical reactions
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.details: Perform and observe combination (water on quicklime), decomposition (heat on ferrous sulphate crystals), displacement (iron nails in copper sulphate), and double displacement reactions (sodium sulphate and barium chloride).

practicals.title: Reactivity series of metals
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.details: Observe the action of Zn, Fe, Cu, and Al on salt solutions (ZnSO4, FeSO4, CuSO4, Al2(SO4)3) to arrange them in decreasing order of reactivity.

practicals.title: Verification of Ohm's Law
practicals.details: Study the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on current (I), determine resistance, and plot a V-I graph.

practicals.title: Equivalent resistance in series and parallel
practicals.details: Determine the equivalent resistance when two resistors are connected in series and in parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata observation
practicals.details: Prepare a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: Respiration experiment (CO2 release)
practicals.details: Experimentally demonstrate that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Properties of acetic acid (ethanoic acid)
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.chapter_name: Carbon and its Compounds / Acids, Bases and Salts
practicals.details: Study the odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, and reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate.

practicals.title: Cleaning capacity of soap in soft and hard water
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.chapter_name: Carbon and its Compounds / Acids, Bases and Salts
practicals.details: Study the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal length determination (Mirror)
practicals.chapter_name: Reflection of light by curved surfaces
practicals.details: Determine the focal length of a concave mirror.

practicals.title: Focal length determination (Lens)
practicals.details: Determine the focal length of a convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object.

practicals.title: Path of light through glass slab
practicals.chapter_name: Refraction / Refraction of light through prism
practicals.details: Trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence, measure angles (incidence, refraction, emergence), and interpret results.

practicals.title: Binary fission in Amoeba and Budding in Yeast/Hydra
practicals.details: Study binary fission in Amoeba, budding in yeast, and budding in Hydra with the help of prepared slides.

practicals.title: Path of light through a prism
practicals.chapter_name: Refraction / Refraction of light through prism
practicals.details: Trace the path of rays of light passing through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Identification of embryo parts in dicot seeds
practicals.details: Identify the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram, or red kidney bean).

projects.title: Portfolio Write-up on Periodic Classification of Elements
projects.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
projects.details: Prepare a brief write-up covering Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, Modern Periodic Table, and properties of metals/non-metals. This is for internal assessment/portfolio credit.

projects.title: Portfolio Write-up on Heredity and Evolution
projects.details: Prepare a brief write-up covering Acquired vs Inherited Traits, Speciation, Tracing Evolutionary Relationships, Fossils, and Human Evolution. This is for internal assessment/portfolio credit.

projects.title: Portfolio Write-up on Electric Effects
projects.details: Prepare a brief write-up covering Motors, Electromagnetic Induction, and Electric Generators. This is for internal assessment/portfolio credit.

marks_distribution: Unit I: Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour - 25 Marks
Unit II: World of Living - 25 Marks
Unit IV: Effects of Current - 13 Marks
competency_weightage: Competencies are assessed at the end of each stage based on Curricular Goals.
Question typology includes: VSA (including objective), Assertion-Reasoning, SA, LA, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based questions.
practical_assessment: List of Experiments includes pH determination, properties of acids/bases, reactivity series, Ohm's law verification, resistance combinations, stomata mount, respiration experiment, acetic acid study, soap cleaning capacity, focal length determination, light refraction through slab/prism, binary fission/budding observation, and embryo identification.
Assessed under Subject Enrichment (5 Marks) for Internal Assessment.
project_assessment: Portfolio assignment on topics not assessed in year-end exam: Periodic Classification of Elements; Heredity and Evolution; Electric Effects of Current.
Students prepare a brief write-up based on reading these chapters, credited under Portfolio (5 Marks).

suspicious_chapter_names: Optics Experiments: Focal Length and Refraction
long_chapter_names_reclassified: Optics Experiments: Focal Length and Refraction
